methods.description: Preparation of whole rock basalt samples for cosmogenic 36Cl measurement, following established procedures. Whole rock analysis was chosen due to the fine-grained nature of the basalt preventing mineral separation.

steps.description: Samples were prepared following previously established procedures (e.g., Barrows et al., 2013).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare whole rock basalt samples for 36Cl analysis according to established protocol.

methods.description: Measurement of major, trace, and chlorine element concentrations in prepared basalt samples using XRF, ICPMS, and isotope dilution. These concentrations are needed for 36Cl production rate calculations and corrections.

steps.description: The concentrations of major target elements for 36 Cl production were determined using X-ray fluorescence (XRF).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure major element concentrations using XRF.

steps.description: The concentrations of trace elements with large neutron capture cross sections (Gd and Sm) and neutron-producing elements (U and Th) were measured by inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICPMS).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure trace element concentrations (Gd, Sm, U, Th) using ICPMS.
outputs: Trace element concentrations (Gd, Sm, U, Th)

steps.description: Chlorine content was determined by isotope dilution.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine total chlorine content using isotope dilution.

methods.description: Measurement of the isotopic ratio of 36Cl to total chlorine in prepared samples using Accelerator Mass Spectrometry (AMS).

steps.description: The isotopic ratios 36 Cl/Cl were measured by accelerator mass spectrometry on the 14UD accelerator at the Australian National University (Fifield et al., 2010).

methods.method_name: Exposure Age Calculation and Error Propagation
methods.description: Calculation of cosmogenic exposure ages from measured 36Cl/Cl ratios and elemental concentrations, including propagation of analytical errors and reporting conventions.

steps.description: Exposure ages are calculated using the scheme of Stone (2000).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate cosmogenic exposure age using Stone (2000) scheme.
inputs: Trace element concentrations (Gd, Sm, U, Th)

steps.description: All analytical errors are fully propagated on individual ages.
steps.searchable_summary: Propagate analytical errors for exposure age calculation.
inputs: Analytical errors from measurements (XRF, ICPMS, Isotope Dilution, AMS)

steps.description: All ages in the text are reported at one standard deviation.
steps.searchable_summary: Report ages with one standard deviation uncertainty.
